marketState: neutral-to-bullish — extreme heat-driven demand offsets bearish seasonality and a storage surplus
summary: Near-term cooling demand is running +28% (1-7D) to +42% (8-16D) above the 5-year norm and above the top of each historical range, with the demand trend still accelerating (+14.6 in 24h) — this is the strongest, most unambiguous signal in the data and is clearly bullish. Front-month ($3.245) sits only modestly above today's fair value ($2.929, band $2.429-$3.429, status SLIGHTLY OVER), and the fair-value curve itself rises to $3.187 by +21 days, converging toward spot, so the current premium is not extreme. Working against the weather tailwind: 16-year seasonality gives just a 19% chance of being higher in 4 weeks with a typical -1.8% 4-week move (though reliability is explicitly flagged Low this week), and storage printed a bearish +13 Bcf surprise with a +175 Bcf surplus to the 5-year average. Managed-money is net short at only the 18th percentile of the past 5 years — stretched positioning that could fuel a short-covering squeeze if the heat persists. Technicals are mixed: 5-minute momentum is bullish but overbought (RSI 68, price above the upper Bollinger band) while daily/weekly EMA structure stays neutral-to-bearish, so price looks short-term stretched even though the fundamental backdrop favors upside.

longTerm.entry: $3.05 - $3.20 (accumulate on weather-driven dips toward fair value)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk (trim size given conflicting seasonal signal)

longTerm.invalidation: Heat anomaly fades back toward the 5y normal range, storage builds keep outpacing forecast, or price breaks and holds below $2.80.

shortTerm.entry: $3.15 - $3.20 on a pullback (chase only above $3.25 if no dip)
shortTerm.rationale: Both weather windows show demand above the historical range max and rising, and the front trades at a slight backwardation to next (-$0.103), consistent with near-term tightness; but 5m/15m RSI and Bollinger readings are overbought, so a pullback toward the fair-value band ($2.93-$3.01) offers a better entry than chasing here.
shortTerm.invalidation: Price closes back below the $2.93-$3.01 fair-value zone, or the 8-16D demand outlook rolls back toward the 5y normal range.
